prompt: A traditional Japanese Edo-period woodblock print (ukiyo-e) in the strict style of Katsushika Hokusai, circa 1830. A lone traveler with a walking stick stepping downward into a deep, undulating mountain valley. The mountain surface is depicted using concentric, swirling topographic contour lines. The image relies purely on flat areas of unmodulated Prussian blue and ochre ink, stark black key-block outlines, strong compositional diagonals, and visible woodgrain texture. No modern 3D shading, no gradients, no text, no modern elements. Authentic 19th-century Japanese print aesthetic.
tradition_approach: Employs Hokusai's compositional diagonals, flat areas of unmodulated Prussian blue and ochre, stark black key-block outlines, visible woodgrain texture, and completely avoids modern perspective shading.
